tavily.answer: On June 18, 2026, an attack on the airport and military airbase in Niger's capital resulted in the deaths of 11 members of the security forces and two civilians. The attack, which involved explosions and sustained gunfire, was repelled by government forces who killed 22 assailants and arrested 20 suspects. This incident follows a similar attack in January by the Islamic State group, which targeted Niger’s drone assets. Additionally, nearly 50 people died of thirst in the Sahara desert after a lorry carrying them broke down while returning from Mali. In a separate development, Niger suspended nine French media outlets, accusing them of threatening public order and national security.
